text:
Once upon a time, there was a wise old owl named Oliver Owlington.

Every dawn, Oliver would sit atop his oak tree overlooking the sleepy village below. He love nothing more than watching the first rays of the sun bathe everything in a golden light.

One day, while reflecting on his quiet, owl-paced life, Oliver thought it would be delightful to teach a lesson about curiosity to the owlet next door.

Oliver fluttered down from his perch.

"Wittolytica," he chirped aloud.

(The little owl looked up with sleepy eyes.)

"Wittoggle," Oliver continued sadly. "It's not wise to spend your days only seeking what already has."

Wittoggle fluttered back into his book.

Oliver continued. "Let us visit the fields," he said. "You might learn something fascinating."

Over the next few days, Oliver and the little owl explored a daisy field. There were so many different colors of blooms!

In the midst of this kaleidoscope
